author: writer-1
author_session_note: Fable experiment-designer role, session 2026-07-10, ratifying the SECOND section-7.3 boundary stop raised by the mechanical runner (same identity mapping as corrections seq 1-3 of this record: author writer-1 is the experiment-designer role, register owner designer-1)
authorized_by: frozen stage discipline of registry/experiments/truthstyle-2x2.json (runner_constraints.stage_discipline: 'the runner never improvises - undecided situations are boundary stops back to Fable') + coordinator escalation 2026-07-10 of the pos-102 ERR_DTS_BLINDING abort; precedent registry/corrections/truthstyle-2x2/1-blinding-audit-clarification.json (the same audit predicate, first false-positive channel) and its own ASM-0360 revisit condition ('any clarified-scan hit ... is a boundary stop back to Fable'), which is exactly what fired
what_happened: In the ASM-0361 restarted campaign opus-runs/20260710T151231Z, judge-p1 and judge-p2 COMPLETED 808/808 with no blinding hit; judge-p3 ABORTED at position 102/808 with ERR_DTS_BLINDING token nsm in events.jsonl. The matched bytes are at offset 1959, inside the Anthropic-generated base62 identifier request_id req_011Cctc1sK9P4SujinnsMiWU (lowercased tail spells ...sujin+nsm+iwu). The item (steamfitter) is byte-clean, the model's whole answer was the single word no, the section-7.2 identity fields are clean, and p1/p2 judged the same item without incident. This contradicts the ASM-0360 premise that non-hex blind tokens appear only via real leakage: Anthropic ids are base62 (req_/msg_ + 24 mixed-case alphanumerics, two per captured p3 attempt), so case-insensitive nsm occurs by arithmetic coincidence with expectation about 1.2 hits per perfectly blind 808-item p3 pass (abort probability about 0.7 under the null). No p3 label was ever assembled, written, or read (data/d-ts-labels contains only the p1/p2 responses; responses are written only at pass END). Unblinding state: pre. The runner again behaved correctly (boundary stop, no self-modification of a pinned control).
ruling: Section 7.3 matching is AMENDED (A1), not weakened: on the two harness-captured surfaces events.jsonl and stderr.log - never on user-prompt.txt - a token match is additionally excluded iff, in the original pre-lowercase bytes, it lies wholly inside the base62 tail of a maximal Anthropic-format vendor id literal (req|msg)_[0-9A-Za-z]{20,48} with lowercase-exact prefix and non-word-adjacent boundaries (runs longer than 48 fail maximality - fail-closed). Uniform over all four pinned tokens; every byte outside qualifying tails is scanned byte-identically to ASM-0360; the 5 tolerated f2b hex-run exclusions are untouched (regression-tested). Re-run shape: judge-p1/p2 passes STAND; ONLY judge-p3 re-runs, fresh full protocol (preflight + all 808 in the frozen p3 seed order) in a NEW run dir under the A1 executor; the void p3 partial (pos-001..102) is retained byte-verbatim as provenance and pos-102 is NOT scrubbed (under A1 it is a listed vendor_id exclusion; the sweep is GREEN over the retained bytes). Full statement, leak-safety argument, rejected alternatives (dropping id fields from the scan surface; scrubbing ids from provenance; changing the pinned section-4.3 capture form), residual-risk census, and normative code are in the ratified amendment section 9 of the clarification doc; operational steps in poc/truthstyle-2x2/p3-rerun-plan.md.

clarification_doc.note: supersedes IN PART the seq-1 doc's section-3 side-statement (non-hex tokens byte-identical) and the section-4/section-5 normative code blocks; sections 1-8 otherwise stand except the section-7 ruling sentence reflowed to a hanging-indent bullet for the epistemic-tag lint (words unchanged); the seq-1 record itself is byte-untouched

poc/truthstyle-2x2/blinding-sweep.py: vendor-id exclusions enumerated from the SAME rdj._vendor_id_tail_spans the abort predicate uses; every exclusion now carries kind (hex_run with surrounding hex run / vendor_id with the full id literal) for auditor inspection (sha256 b53b6d7a2f2e2cfe684c18eda896c73cce3390896aa4584287e96ad7960bdcea). GREEN still requires zero clarified hits.

P3-ONLY RE-RUN: p1/p2 passes of 20260710T151231Z stand; fresh p3 preflight + main in a new run dir; executor shas recorded there before launch; p1/p2 summary/preflight files copied byte-identical for finalize; A1 sweep GREEN required over BOTH run dirs before the d-ts-labels ops amendment.

NO frozen or staged byte changed: registry/experiments/truthstyle-2x2.json, all 8 harness-manifest files (judges-invocation.md and the pinned section-4.3 p3 capture form included), amendments 1-3, corrections 1-3, and the pinned analysis are byte-identical before and after this amendment.
selftest: 34/34 checks PASS 2026-07-10 via the committed re-runnable harness poc/truthstyle-2x2/blinding-selftest-a1.py (sha256 ceb046bcee41097a7339132151ea56ae4977bfd7e391fc24d6562e17f37a2061): real pos-102 excluded; 5 f2b hex-run regressions still excluded; 11 real-leak shapes (answer-text echo, path fragments, delimited spellings, short/long/embedded/uppercase/wrong-prefix id lookalikes, delimiter-adjacent tokens) all still trip; id-shaped bytes in user-prompt.txt still trip; end-to-end sweep GREEN on a synthetic mini run dir with both exclusion kinds listed; read-only sweep-equivalent walk over 20260710T151231Z yields zero clarified hits and exactly 6 exclusions (5 f2b hex-run + 1 nsm vendor-id at pos-102)
consumer_note: The Codex GATE-A auditor can re-derive this ruling mechanically: all attempt artifacts of both the void run and the retained p3 partial are byte-verbatim on disk, so the strict scan (fires on the pos-102 request_id coincidence), the ASM-0360 scan (also fires: nsm is not hex-embeddable), and the A1 scan (fires on nothing) can each be independently re-run over the same bytes.
